mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, PDGFR and PKC signaling kinases
additional_notes: Also known as PKC412; approved for FLT3‑mutated AML; active at low nanomolar concentrations in FLT3‑ITD AML lines such as MOLM13

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, RAF, VEGFR and PDGFR kinases
additional_notes: Used off‑label in FLT3‑ITD AML; strong activity in MOLM13 and MV4‑11

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, PDGFR and VEGFR kinases
additional_notes: Reported anti‑proliferative effects in FLT3‑ITD AML models

mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL, FLT3 and FGFR kinases
additional_notes: Potent FLT3 inhibitor including some TKD mutants; active in FLT3‑ITD AML cell lines

mechanism: Inhibits SRC and ABL family kinases
additional_notes: Shows anti‑leukemic activity in subsets of AML, including some FLT3‑ITD models via downstream signaling effects

mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL, KIT and PDGFR kinases
additional_notes: Shown to inhibit proliferation in some AML cell lines, especially with activated KIT/PDGFR pathways

mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL, KIT and PDGFR kinases
additional_notes: Less potent in FLT3‑ITD AML; useful benchmark for KIT/PDGFR dependency in MOLM13

mechanism: Selectively inhibits the anti‑apoptotic protein BCL‑2
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑199; approved for AML in combination regimens; MOLM13 is typically BCL‑2‑dependent and sensitive

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2, blocking JAK‑STAT signaling
additional_notes: Used to probe JAK‑STAT pathway contribution to AML cell survival, including in MOLM13

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK3, modulating cytokine receptor signaling
additional_notes: Approved for rheumatoid arthritis; some JAK‑dependent leukemias show sensitivity

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1, reducing PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway signaling
additional_notes: mTOR inhibition reduces AML proliferation and can synergize with FLT3 or BCL‑2 inhibitors

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1 via FKBP12‑rapamycin complex
additional_notes: Also known as Rapamycin; classic mTOR inhibitor with anti‑proliferative effects in AML cell lines

mechanism: Activates AMPK and inhibits mitochondrial complex I, impairing oxidative phosphorylation
additional_notes: Antidiabetic drug; at high micromolar to low millimolar concentrations can reduce mitochondrial function and AML proliferation

mechanism: Inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase and, with copper, forms complexes that generate ROS and inhibit proteasome/NPL4
additional_notes: Often tested with added Cu (e.g., 1–5 µM); AML stem/progenitor cells are often ALDH‑high; active in FLT3‑ITD AML lines

mechanism: Inhibits class I and IIa histone deacetylases, altering chromatin and gene expression
additional_notes: Used clinically as an anticonvulsant; has differentiation‑inducing and cytostatic effects in AML

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, promoting acetylation and transcriptional reprogramming
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; approved for CTCL; shows potent anti‑proliferative and pro‑apoptotic effects in AML, including FLT3‑ITD models

mechanism: Broadly inhibits class I, II and IV histone deacetylases
additional_notes: Very potent HDAC inhibitor with reported low‑nanomolar activity in AML cell lines

mechanism: Blocks lysosomal acidification and autophagic flux
additional_notes: Antimalarial drug; hydroxychloroquine is a related alternative; autophagy inhibition can enhance AML cell death and TKI responses

mechanism: Binds cereblon to modulate E3 ubiquitin ligase activity and induce degradation of targets such as IKZF1/3
additional_notes: Lenalidomide is a related IMiD; both are cereblon modulators with immunomodulatory and anti‑leukemic effects in some AML subsets

mechanism: Uncouples oxidative phosphorylation and inhibits signaling pathways including Wnt/β‑catenin, STAT3 and NF‑κB
additional_notes: Repurposed anthelmintic with published anti‑leukemic and anti‑stem‑cell activity in AML; perturbs mitochondrial metabolism and survival signaling
